Together, Asha and Star confront a most formidable foe the ruler of Rosas, King Magnifico to save her community and prove that when the will of one courageous human connects with the magic of the stars, wondrous things can happen.

Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein). For school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program.

events.title: Hands-On Introduction to Robots Sensors Programming
events.description: Gain hands-on experience on how robots use their sensors to work using Scribbler Robots, then test how their programming allows these robots to respond to their environments. This workshop starts by showing why and how NASA is using robots to explore Mars and ends with showing how programming allows robots and non-intended devices to actually work together.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Oat Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classics ingredients including oat milk and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Author Visit: Jane and the Blue Willow Princess with Catherine Little
events.description: “On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​” Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.

events.title: Explorers Club

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Board Games for Kids
events.description: Jenga! Monopoly! Ticket to Ride! And More! Spend the afternoon playing different board games with your friends!  Snacks are offered during this program for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: Hands-on K'NEX 3-D Construction & Destruction Challenge
events.description: Have fun and collaborate to imagine, design, and construct lightweight, high‑strength 3‑D bridges using supplied K’NEX materials. Then evaluate the structures through controlled load testing using a specialized “Bridge‑Crusher” rig, which reveals each bridge’s performance through its strength‑to‑weight ratio. Through multiple design iterations, participants observe how specific modifications influence real‑world structural strength. Gain an understanding of applied science and engineering princi

events.title: Vegetarian Sushi Making Workshop for Kids with Chef Sang Kim
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of sushi making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best sushi creations. events.title: Cosplay Meetup
events.description: Drop in to work on your cosplay outfits, and meet others who are also getting ready for Anime North and Fan Expo Calling all cosplayers! Whether you have many cosplays under your belt, or you are excited to try the hobby for the first time, we invite you to join us at North York Central Library! We will be hosting a cosplayer meetup, where enthusiasts can work on their cosplays, share ideas, and connect with each other! Cosplayers will have access to a working space equipped with fabrication equ

events.title: FRESH Movie Saturdays
events.description: This program helps people stay active, make friends, learn new skills, and have fun. Meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighbourhood! Come join us every Saturday for screenings of your favourite movies. In addition to this, enjoy cuddles with a therapy dog! Therapy dog Goose visits Fort York Branch on alternate Saturdays from 11:00AM-12:00PM. We'll be showing the following films: May 2-Wonder May 9-Stuart Little May

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. DROP IN Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D pr

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Creator: Mother's Day 3D Design
events.description: To celebrate Mother’s Day. Let’s be creative and make a special 3D design gift for your mom. In this class, we will work together to make a personalized cardholder with TinkerCad. Adult, Children 9+

events.title: Mother's Day Mug Making (Ages 13 and up)
events.description: Looking for a Last minute Mother's Day gift for mom? Join us to design your gift with Cricut Design Space. Once your design is done, we will cut your design using the Cricut Machine and Heat press your designs onto the Mug.  Ages 8 - 12 at 10:30 - 12 pm  Ages 13 and up at 2:00 - 3:00 pm  Mouse and Keyboard skills are a must!  Registration is required. Registration opens 1 week before class. (April 25th at 9 am)  Information Collection Notice:  The personal

events.title: Saturday Movies: Mulan
events.description: Celebrating Heritage Asian Month, Hillcrest branch will screen Disney's Mulan (2020), starring Yifei Liu and Gong Li.  To keep her ailing father from serving in the Imperial Army, a fearless young woman disguises herself as a man and battles northern invaders in China.  Drop-in. No registration. Everyone is welcome.

events.title: Vegetarian Sushi Making Workshop with Chef Sang Kim
events.description: TV host and celebrity Chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of sushi making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best sushi creations.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL Digital Innovation Hubs.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday STEAM Stations
events.description: Build, create, learn & play at various stations including Snap Circuits, LEGO, KEVA Planks, Connectors, & Colouring! This is a program for kids, teens, & families.  All are welcome!  Drop-in, no registration required. Please note: there is no program on Sun May 17, as TPL will be closed for observation of the Victoria Day holiday.

events.title: Traditional Lion Dance
events.description: The Chinatown Community Lion Dance Group performs the traditional Lion Dance with drumming, cymbals and the two Chinese Lions operated by a team of 4. After a talk about the history and cultural significance of the dance, they will parade around the space and interact with the audience. Drop in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Spring Tumbler Craft Lab

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

In this follow up course we will take our understanding of data structures to the next level as well as looking into the next stages of error handling (including making some custom error messages) and extending our knowledge of input/output to more than just the terminal. events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. You'll learn how to guide AI tools to serve your vision rather than replace it, and we'll cover Canadian copyright guidelines to ensure the music you

events.title: How to Become a Volunteer
events.description: Interested in learning more about volunteering opportunities in Toronto? This session is intended for the volunteer seekers who are new to volunteering, or in need to understand the process of formal volunteering. Join us to learn how and where to search for opportunities that best match your interests and what to expect after you apply for a volunteer role. This session caters to all demographics. Presented by Volunteer Toronto .

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. You'll also hear about landmark legal cases - both su

Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Rulers in Space: Measuring Distances in the Universe
events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? Grab your cosmic ruler and embark on an exploration of the methods used to infer distances in the Universe — the result of centuries of innovative thinking by countless generations of astronomers. events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around  the Branch Tour
events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood walk. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required.
